title: APK package manager not found in Docker container
symptom: When trying to install packages in a Docker container, the command 'apk' returns 'sh: apk: not found'. This indicates that the Alpine package manager is not available in the hardened image.
applies_when: Using a hardened Docker image of n8n
Attempting to install packages using apk
not_this_if: Using a standard Docker image that includes apk
root_cause: The hardened n8n Docker image removes the apk package manager for security reasons, preventing users from installing additional packages directly within the container.
fix_steps: Download the static apk binary using wget: wget https://gitlab.alpinelinux.org/api/v4/projects/5/packages/generic/v2.14.4/x86_64/apk.static
Make the apk binary executable: chmod +x apk.static
Run the apk binary to install apk-tools: ./apk.static -X http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/main -U --allow-untrusted --initdb add apk-tools
agent_instruction: Download and install the apk package manager in your Docker container using the provided commands.
safety_notes: This installation will be undone every time you down your Docker compose or remove the container.
